identify_breaking_api.expected_solution: Agent must identify the breaking change in producer `protocolbuffers/protobuf (google.golang.org/protobuf)` introduced by grpc/grpc-go#6919. The IMPACT_REPORT.md must name the legacy symbols (status.Details(), ptypes.UnmarshalAny, proto.MessageV1, and proto.MessageV2) and their replacements ((none)), and point to producer-side artifacts (encoding/proto/proto.go, internal/status/status.go, internal/binarylog/method_logger.go, internal/binarylog/sink.go, internal/pretty/pretty.go, balancer/grpclb/grpclb.go, balancer/grpclb/grpclb_remote_balancer.go, internal/transport/handler_server.go, internal/transport/http2_server.go, and interop/test_utils.go).
evaluation_criteria: Names the producer repository `protocolbuffers/protobuf (google.golang.org/protobuf)` and the breaking PR/commit (grpc/grpc-go#6919)
Lists the deprecated/removed symbols: status.Details(), ptypes.UnmarshalAny, proto.MessageV1, and proto.MessageV2

References at least one producer-side file (basename: proto.go, status.go, method_logger.go, sink.go, pretty.go, grpclb.go, grpclb_remote_balancer.go, handler_server.go, http2_server.go, and test_utils.go)

find_direct_consumers.expected_solution: Agent must enumerate every consumer file in grpc/grpc-go that uses the deprecated symbols (status.Details(), ptypes.UnmarshalAny, proto.MessageV1, and proto.MessageV2). Per ground_truth: grpc-go: internal/status/status.go, status/status_ext_test.go, internal/testutils/status_equal.go. The report must cite each consumer file by name and indicate which deprecated symbol it depends on.
evaluation_criteria: Cites each consumer file by name (basenames: status.go, status_ext_test.go, and status_equal.go)
For each cited consumer, names which deprecated symbol from status.Details(), ptypes.UnmarshalAny, proto.MessageV1, and proto.MessageV2 it uses
Distinguishes files with compile-time dependencies from files that only forward/proxy the symbol

trace_transitive_impact.expected_solution: Agent must trace transitive impact: which downstream code paths in grpc/grpc-go indirectly observe the producer change. The answer must identify forwarding/middleware/interceptor code that propagates the affected types and explain why the type or contract change cascades beyond the directly-importing files. Reference the consumer fix PR(s) (grpc/grpc-go#7724) where applicable.
evaluation_criteria: Identifies at least one transitive code path beyond the direct importers (interceptor/middleware/wrapper)
Explains the propagation mechanism (type changes, signature changes, behavior drift) for status.Details(), ptypes.UnmarshalAny, proto.MessageV1, and proto.MessageV2
Cross-references the consumer-side fix PR(s) (grpc/grpc-go#7724) or the migration pattern they encode

classify_breakage.expected_solution: Agent must classify each affected file by failure mode. The ground-truth breakage type is `runtime_behavior`. For `compile_and_runtime` cases the report must identify both compile-time breakage (undefined functions, signature mismatches) and runtime behavior changes (silent metadata loss, semantic shifts). For `compile_only` or `runtime_only` cases, justify why only one class applies.
evaluation_criteria: Distinguishes compile-time failures (undefined functions, type errors) from runtime behavior changes (silent semantics drift)
Identifies at least one specific consumer file per failure class consistent with the classification
